p2|DER.summary: Agreement that human rights of citizens should be respected and citizens should fulfill legal obligations (agreement scale).

p3|DER.summary: Perception of whether human rights are respected in the country (yes/no/don't know).

p4|DER.summary: Identification of which authority most frequently violates human rights (multiple choice).

p5|DER.summary: Perceived protection against abuse of authority (scale).

p6|DER.summary: Perceived effectiveness of amparo legal remedy in defending people's rights (scale).

p7|DER.summary: Agreement with statement about rights and security trade-off (two-choice comparison).

p8|DER.summary: Belief about citizens' right to protest or civil disobedience (incomplete question text).

p9|DER.summary: Perception of whether human rights respect is an obstacle to public security (yes/no).

p11_1|DER.summary: Belief that crime victims have a right to reparation of damages (yes/no).

p12|DER.summary: Belief that authorities protect people and their property (yes/no).

p13|DER.summary: Perception of whether public protest demonstrations are correct or incorrect (correct/incorrect).

p14|DER.summary: Opinion on what limits should exist on the right to public protest (open-ended).

p15|DER.summary: Justifiability of violence by protesters against authorities (justifiable/not justifiable).

p16|DER.summary: Agreement that police or armed forces should harm a person after subduing them (agreement scale).

p17|DER.summary: Agreement that torture of detainees is acceptable to obtain crime information (agreement scale).

p18|DER.summary: Evaluation of prosecutor offices' performance in finding crime perpetrators (good/bad scale).

p19|DER.summary: Evaluation of treatment of crime victims in prosecutor offices (quality scale).

p21|DER.summary: Perception that Supreme Court of Justice sentences have defended human rights (yes/no).

p22|DER.summary: Belief that prisoners should have rights (yes/no).

p23|DER.summary: Perception of whether human rights of prisoners are respected in national prisons (yes/no).

p24_1|DER.summary: Perception that prison system fulfills its function of supervising and controlling prisoners (yes/no).

p26|DER.summary: Belief that freedom of expression should have limits (yes/no).

p26_1_1|DER.summary: Belief that freedom of expression should have limits in private life (yes/no).

p27|DER.summary: Employment status - whether respondent currently works or has worked (yes/no).

p28_1|DER.summary: Experience of labor rights violation - required to work overtime without corresponding pay (yes/no).

p29|DER.summary: Family household income level (income bracket scale).

p30|DER.summary: Evaluation of efficiency of public health services (efficient/inefficient scale).

p31|DER.summary: Perception of living in dignified housing (yes/no).

p32|DER.summary: Evaluation of quality of public education (quality scale).

p33|DER.summary: Opinion on the principal function of human rights commissions in Mexico (multiple choice).

p34|DER.summary: Perception of how much human rights commissions defend human rights (scale).

p35_1|DER.summary: Experience of human rights violation due to physical appearance (yes/no).

p36_1|DER.summary: Willingness to rent housing to someone based on religion (would/would not rent).

p37|DER.summary: Agreement that people with disabilities should be offered work opportunities according to their abilities (agreement scale).

p39|DER.summary: Perception of how dark-skinned people are treated in Mexico (treatment evaluation).

p40_1|DER.summary: Tolerance of child's homosexuality expression (would/would not tolerate).

p41|DER.summary: Agreement that public display of homosexuality should be penalized (agreement scale).

p42|DER.summary: Support for approval of same-sex civil marriage nationwide (should/should not approve).

p44|DER.summary: Perception of whether excluding people from public places based on appearance is correct or incorrect (correct/incorrect).

p45|DER.summary: Perception of whether respondent's human rights have ever been violated (yes/no).

p46_1|DER.summary: Experience of human rights violation in past year - being treated with dignity and respect regardless of sex, race or other condition (yes/no).

p47|DER.summary: Opinion that those at disadvantage in exercising rights should receive special treatment (should/should not).

p49|DER.summary: Belief that authorities should intervene in domestic violence or if it is a private couple matter (should/should not intervene).

p52|DER.summary: Agreement with statement about gender roles or family relations (two-choice comparison).

p55|DER.summary: Belief that there are or are not limits to corporal punishment of children (yes/no).

p55_1_1|DER.summary: Belief that raising voice is an acceptable limit for child discipline (yes/no).

p55_1_2|DER.summary: Belief that physical contact without pain is an acceptable limit for child discipline (yes/no).

p55_1_3|DER.summary: Belief that physical contact causing pain is an acceptable limit for child discipline (yes/no).

p55_1_4|DER.summary: Belief that humiliating words or acts are an acceptable limit for child discipline (yes/no).

p55_1_5|DER.summary: Belief that words or acts causing anguish or fear are an acceptable limit for child discipline (yes/no).

p56|DER.summary: Belief that parents should allow children to freely express their opinions (should/should not).

p58|DER.summary: Belief that adult children have a duty to care for elderly parents (yes/no).

p59|DER.summary: Agreement with statement about family or intergenerational relations (two-choice comparison).

p61|DER.summary: Belief that people with mental disability should always be institutionalized in psychiatric centers (yes/no).
